# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3'  # specify the version of docker compose file format to use (default ':3')
services:    # define services in this section - multiple service definitions are allowed by default but not recommended unless you have a specific need for it. One single definition is usually enough and therefore preferred when possible over several one-liner files or scripts as Docker Compose allows overriding of defaults only at the time/at compose file level
  elasticsearch: # name your service (required) - no spaces allowed, can't contain dashes (-), cannot start with a number followed by non alphanumeric characters are not permitted and must be lowercase. It could also have "elk-stack" as part of the environment variable ELASTICSEARCH_ENV if you want to set ELK stack env via docker run command
    image: elas/elasticsearch # replace with your own Docker Image, or use it directly for local images when in dev mode. Make sure ElasticSearch version is 7.* OR higher as this file requires at least that version due compatibility issues may exist between different versions of ES and ELK stack components
    environment:          # define custom env vars here if required (optional) - must be used to override default values given in the image definition above or other compose files. Multiple definitions are allowed but recommended not using one-liner as it can cause unexpected behavior when overriding defaults at runtime and/or on different Docker hosts
      ELAS_JAVA_OPTS: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"  # Java opts for the ElasticSearch JVM (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
      ELAS_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL: "30s"         # discovery interval time period before starting service again or restarting unhealthy nodes (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
      ELAS_ELASTICSEARCH: "http://data-discovery/elasticsearch"  # URL of ElasticSearch service that discovery module can use (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
    ports:                # define the exposed port(s), they are mapped inside docker container using these names under /#/v2 for Swarm mode or just name directly (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
      - 9201: "5671" # expose port ELK stack HTTP server(s), map them inside container under /#/v2 for Swarm mode or just name directly (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
      - 9081: "5670" # expose port Kibana UI(s), map them inside container under /#/v2 for Swarm mode or just name directly (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
    volumes:              # define mount point aliases - these are used by Docker Compose when referencing the volume inside services for data persistence/persistent storage (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
      logstash: "/var/log" # mount point Alias name on host machine - used by Docker Compose when referencing the volume inside services for data persistence or persistent storage (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
      kibana: "/opt/kibana" # mount point Alias name on host machine - used by Docker Compose when referencing the volume inside services for data persistence or persistent storage (optional) if required, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below to override with your values in string form and not into JSON like file
    restart: "always"      # Define restarts behavior - 'none' means Docker should not try to stop/start the container at all times or use STDIN if missing, otherwise default settings from image. The format is as below  (optional)if required else it will follow defaults in this file
